figures.caption: Table 4: Test accuracy on the CIFAR-10 dataset. We test two active ratios on each dataset. On each setup, we test two Dirichlet splittings, and each result tests 3 times. The table reports results on ResNet-18-GN (upper) and VGG-11 (lower).
Table 10: Some abbreviations of the used terms in the proof of the optimization process.

Table 6: We incorporate the relaxed initialization (RI) into the different benchmarks to test their improvements on the ResNet-18-GN model on the CIFAR-10 dataset under the same settings and hyperparameters selections.
Table 2: Summary of the convergence bound of our proposed FedInit method.
Table 8: Acceleration ratio of the communication rounds and wall-clock time of the ResNet18-GN model on CIFAR-10 dataset.

Figure 2: The accuracy and divergence of different β. (a) and (b) are tested with FedAvg + RI (FedInit), while (c) and (d) for SCAFFOLD + RI. (a) and (c) are tested on the Dir-0.6 10%-100 setups, while (b) and (d) for Dir-0.1 5%-200 setups.

Table 5: Test accuracy on the CIFAR-10/100 dataset. We test two active ratios on each dataset. On each setup, we test two Dirichlet splittings, and each result tests 3 times. The table reports results on ResNet-18-GN (upper) and VGG-11 (lower).
Figure 1: Sensitivity studies of local intervals K and relaxed coefficient β of the FedInit method on CIFAR-10. To fairly compare their efficiency.
